behavior_in_task_description.reason: instruction.md names the failing test files, function names (test_phi3_mini_*, test_sdpa_can_dispatch_on_flash), and the required model families (Pi0, parakeet_encoder, parakeet_ctc, Evolla) plus the preserved skip entries.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes what's broken (argument-related errors in generate, SDPA dispatch failures) without specifying the fix like **inputs or the exact skip-list strings.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: test_phi3_generate_no_bare_inputs accepts any fix that doesn't pass bare 'inputs' positionally; SDPA test uses substring matching on model_type keys, which are API-level identifiers any valid fix must use.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests use AST parsing (ast.walk, ast.parse) and subprocess calls (ruff, git diff) rather than raw string matching, though limited to static analysis due to GPU/weight constraints as documented.

test_not_tautological.reason: test_phi3_generate_no_bare_inputs requires >=4 generate() calls preserved AND no bare inputs; test_sdpa_skip_new_models requires specific literal strings; a stub removing tests would fail test_phi3_test_methods_intact.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All behaviors required (phi3 fix, SDPA skip additions, preservation of existing skips) are tested.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are grouped by section headers (Gates, Fail-to-pass, Pass-to-pass, Anti-stub, Config-derived) with clear comments indicating tier and purpose.

structured_data_schema.reason: Task is code-fix, not structured data output.

no_typos.reason: File paths and names match (tests/models/phi3/test_modeling_phi3.py, tests/test_modeling_common.py, test_sdpa_can_dispatch_on_flash).

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction points to files to investigate but doesn't name the buggy line or exact fix pattern; localization within files still required.
